sections.paragraph:
ἐγκαλέω (ἐν + καλέω; s. ἔγκλημα) impv. 3 pl. ἐγκαλείτωσαν; impf. ἐνεκάλουν; fut. ἐγκαλέσω; 1 aor. ἐνεκάλεσα LXX. Pass. fut. ptc. gen. pl. ἐγκληθησομένων (Ath., R. 77, 17); 1 aor. 3 sg. ἐνεκλήθη (Just., A II, 10, 5) (Soph., X., Pla.+). Legal t.t. bring charges against, accuse τινί (Soph., Pla. et al.; PEleph 1, 7; Sir 46:19; Wsd 12:12; Jos., C. Ap. 2, 138; w. the cause as obj. τῇ αὐτῶν κακίᾳ ἐγκαλεῖτε Just., D. 30, 1) someone Ac 19:38 (ἐ. ἀλλήλοις as PHib 96 [s. below]; Jos., Ant. 3, 213); 23:28; also ἐ. κατά τινος Ro 8:33. Pass. (Just., A II, 10, 5 [here w. acc.] Σωκράτης τὰ αὐτὰ ἡμῖν ἐνεκλήθη Socrates experienced the same kind of charges that are levelled against us): περί τινος because of someth. (the act. Diod S 11, 83, 3; UPZ 63, 9 [158 b.c.]; PHamb 25, 3; Jos., Ant. 12, 172) περὶ ζητημάτων τοῦ νόμου αὐτῶν because of questions concerning their law Ac 23:29; because of a hope 26:7. τινός because of someth. (not earlier than IV b.c. [Demades 61 Blass] and rare; Plut., Aristid. 324 [10, 7]; Cass. Dio 58, 4 ἀσεβείας ἐς τὸν Τιβέριον ἐγκληθείς; Jos., Ant. 5, 56; B-D-F §178) ἐ. στάσεως 19:40; περὶ πάντων ὧν ἐγκαλοῦμαι on all the charges made against me 26:2 (PHib 96, 6; 22 [259 b.c.] περὶ ὧν ἐνεκάλεσαν ἀλλήλοις; cp. OGI 229, 42).—B. 1439. Renehan ’75, 75. M-M. TW.

impv. impv. = imperative

  1. pl. = plural

impf. impf. = imperfect

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

ptc. ptc. = participle

gen. gen. = genitive

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

  1. sg. = singular

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PEleph PEleph = Elephantine-Papyri—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

obj. obj. = object, objective

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

PHib PHib = The Hibeh Papyri I–II—List 4

acc. acc. = accusative

someth. someth. = something

act. act. = active

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

PHamb PHamb = Griechische Papyrusurkunden der Hamburger Staats- und Universitätsbibliothek—List 4

Demades Demades , IV b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

Renehan Renehan = RR., Greek Lexicographical Notes—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 273
